what_happened: Added dependency-review-action security-scan job to GitHub Actions PR workflow
why: The dependency-review-action requires only 'contents: read' permission and the pull_request trigger — both already present in the workflow. No node_modules install needed since the action operates on manifest/lock file diffs from the GitHub API.
fix_applied: Added a standalone security-scan job using actions/dependency-review-action@v4 with fail-on-severity: high (covers high and critical)
pattern_to_remember: actions/dependency-review-action only needs checkout + the action step itself — no pnpm/node setup required. Set fail-on-severity: high to block both high and critical vulnerabilities. The action works on pull_request triggers only (compares base vs head manifests via GitHub API).
